name: faultline
description: Private AI memory system — stores facts in a knowledge graph and recalls them across conversations.

tools.name: recall_memory
tools.description: Recall what you know about the user or a topic from memory. Call this at the start of any turn where the user's message touches on something you might already know about them.

tools.name: remember_facts
tools.description: Store facts from the conversation OR correct/update a prior fact. Correction signals: 'actually X is Y', 'X is now Y not Z', 'I meant X', any update to a previously stated value.

tools.name: learn_facts
tools.description: Ingest structured ontological statements — 'X is a subclass of Y', 'X is an instance of Y', 'X is a part of Y'. Maps how concepts relate.

tools.name: retract_fact
tools.description: Remove a fact from memory. Use ONLY for deletions — 'forget that', 'remove', 'erase'. Do NOT use for corrections — use remember_facts instead.

api_url.title: FaultLine MCP URL
api_url.description: HTTP endpoint for the FaultLine MCP server. Default: http://localhost:8002. Change the host if FaultLine runs on another machine (e.g., http://192.168.1.50:8002).

user_id.title: User ID
user_id.description: Your FaultLine user UUID. This isolates your memory from other users. Format: xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx. Generate one with: python -c "import uuid; print(uuid.uuid4())". If you also use OpenWebUI, use the same UUID from OpenWebUI Settings > Account so both clients share one memory store.

mcp_api_key.title: MCP API Key
mcp_api_key.description: Bearer token for MCP authentication. Must match the MCP_API_KEY value in your Docker .env file.
